← the Atlas

arivu — terminal awareness as an ephemeral surface on kaval

Features· budding ·accepted ·JUN 21, 2026

Repeat the kaval decoupling one level up. The awareness sensors (git · PR · agent · foreground) become a generic library — kolu runs it in-process locally (writing terminalMetadata directly), and an ephemeral daemon (arivu) on top of kaval serves the same awareness over ssh for remote terminals, mirrored + folded into kolu. One library, two homes. arivu + arivu-tui ship standalone first; the kolu fold lives in remote-terminals R7.

Contents

  • The core model
  • One library, two homes — and why only remote folds
  • The standalone tool — arivu + arivu-tui
  • The R4 tree — shipped end-to-end
  • R4.5 — the fleet board
  • Why this stays a TUI, and what is electricity vs. a leaf
  • Why Bun, contained to the viewer
  • History

kaval decoupled the PTY out of kolu into a standalone, minimal, durable daemon. This note makes the same move one level up for the part kolu kept tangling: terminal awareness (git branch/dirty, PR status, agent detection, foreground). A new daemon — arivu (Tamil அறிவு, “the faculty of knowing”; sibling to kaval = guard and odu = run) — sits on top of kaval, runs the sensors, and exposes the result as one typed @kolu/surface collection. One sensor library, two homes: locally kolu runs it in-process and writes terminalMetadata straight through the existing mutators (no surface, no fold); remotely the arivu daemon serves the same awareness over ssh, which kolu mirrors and folds in. Clients keep reading the one terminalMetadata collection they always did.

The core model

Three claims, each verified against the shipped code:

  • The unit is a generic awareness value, not kolu’s record. arivu exposes Collection<TerminalId, AwarenessValue> — exactly cwd · git · lastAgentCommand · lastActivityAt plus pr · agent · foreground. That schema lives in @kolu/terminal-awareness, built from the vendor-neutral anyagent / anyforge / kolu-git shapes with no kolu content — not even location (no sensor can know its own kolu-side hostId). Kolu’s TerminalServerMetadata is AwarenessValue plus location and UI fields: built on it, never carved from it.
  • kaval stays byte-for-byte minimal. arivu dials kaval as a ptyHostSurface client for the four taps + getScreenText; it adds zero awareness/git/gh logic to kaval. A layer above, never a fork.
  • Ephemerality is the simplifier. Awareness never has to survive a restart — it’s re-derivable from live taps + current host fs. So arivu sheds all of kaval’s durability machinery: no single-instance lock, no spawn/cleanup, no persisted list (it borrows kaval’s terminal.list), no adoption or reconnect. Every (re)start just re-runs the sensors from now.

One library, two homes — and why only remote folds

Local and remote share the sensor library and one schema — but they are genuinely different paths, not “only the transport.” The surface and the fold exist only where awareness crosses a process boundary:

  • Local → in-process, no surface. kolu-server runs the sensors and writes terminalMetadata directly. Same process = nothing to serve, nothing to merge; wrapping it in an in-process surface is the fictional boundary rejected in #1406. Local stays this way for good — in-process sensors are always the running build, fresh by construction. Even after remote ships, local does not switch to dialing a local arivu “for consistency” (pure ceremony to move data within one process). The consistency that matters — same library, schema, contract — is already there.
  • Remote → serve (arivu), then mirror + fold (kolu). arivu is Nix-provisioned over ssh (kaval’s staleKey recipe), runs the identical sensors, and serves the slice over stdioLink. The kolu-side mirror + fold is not arivu’s concern — it is remote-terminals R7’s, and the fold’s costs live there.

So the asymmetry is real and confined to the wire: local is closure → mutate; remote is closure → frame → serve → mirror → fold. They share the invariant sensor library and one schema. The one new artifact is the host-side hooks impl that reifies each mutate closure into a serialized slice frame — a wire carries values, not closures.

mirrorRemoteSurface has graduated ( #1497 ). It is now the one public mirror in @kolu/surface — the spec-driven dual of implementSurface that drives every primitive of a served surface into caller-supplied sinks; mirrorRemoteCollection is demoted to its private per-key engine (“you don’t sell half a house”). The trigger was arivu growing a real activity stream (the green dot) — the second stream-bearing consumer the graduation waited for. It still does not dissolve kolu’s R7 fold (irreducible domain logic); the fold simply consumes mirrorRemoteSurface. As a @kolu/surface API change it rode surface.md’s drishti merge-gate.

The standalone tool — arivu + arivu-tui

Like kaval, the daemon is a deliverable in its own right, shipped and proven before kolu touches it. Where kaval-tui shows what’s running in each PTY, arivu-tui shows what each terminal is in — a “what is every agent doing, across every repo” dashboard with zero kolu-server, dialable over ssh against a prod box.

arivu-tui · ssh nix@prod
$ arivu-tui --host prod
ID BRANCH DIRTY PR AGENT FOREGROUND
a3f1 feat/dial-ssh ● #1412 ✓ claude · working node
b7c2 master · — codex · waiting codex
c9d4 fix/fold ● #1408 ✗ — nvim
# derived intelligence over the raw PTY — a future TUI-kolu consumes the same daemon

It ships self-contained: the sensors (@kolu/terminal-awareness/sensors.ts) import nothing from kolu but a logger, and the host runtime deps are just node · git · gh (SQLite via Node’s built-in node:sqlite — no native addon), so the bin travels cleanly over ssh. Define the contract once and the consumers fall out of one schema — the daemon serves it, the TUI reads it, and remotely kolu’s mirror folds it.

The R4 tree — shipped end-to-end

The standalone arivu story (extract → daemon → remote viewer → Bun → OpenTUI → live fleet → live activity) is complete. The kolu fold — kolu dialing this host’s arivu over a long-lived HostSession, mirroring it, and folding it into its own canvas — is not arivu’s job; it lives in the parent remote-terminals roadmap as R7, gated on the remote dial. And the fs/git surface a remote Code tab needs is roadmapped there as R6: it becomes a shared library the way the sensors already are (@kolu/terminal-awareness), so kolu runs it in-process locally and arivu hosts the same library remotely — one implementation, never a second fs/git inside arivu. arivu just points there.

PhaseShips #1413 etc.
R4.1 refactorextract @kolu/terminal-awareness — sensors + generic schemas, off kolu-common; the schema home inverts (kolu-common now imports the schemas and adds location). Behaviour-preserving — green CI is the proof. #1413
R4.2 refactorfinish the provider → adapter rename through the anyagent/anyforge leaves (symbols, files, exports — one adapter spine). The live Watcher handle + wire provider discriminant stay. Behaviour-preserving. #1419
R4.3 featurearivu + arivu-tui standalone — the daemon dials kaval and serves the awareness surface; the TUI reads it. Zero kolu-server. --stdio is the seam the ssh dial speaks to. #1428
R4.4 featurearivu-tui --host — dial + Nix-provision a remote arivu over ssh, render the same dashboard. The shared one-shot dial graduated to @kolu/surface-nix-host’s dialAgentOnce (a version-cell read is arivu’s connectivity probe). Zero kolu-server. #1439
R4.5 featurethe fleet dashboard — see below. #1470 · #1479 · #1486 · #1497

R4.5 — the fleet board

The user’s framing made literal — “what is every agent doing, across every repo, across every machine” — as a dashboard you leave open on a second monitor: glance over from the game and a colour you can read across the room tells you whether any agent is blocked on you, and where. It stays a TUI, runs zero kolu-server, and shipped as four steps:

  • R4.5.1 — Bun runtime #1470 . Re-platform arivu-tui onto Bun via drishti’s bun2nix recipe — pinned via npins, resolved with builtins.getFlake (the odu path, so kolu’s zero-flake-input rule holds), bun.lock + autogenerated bun.nix kept outside the pnpm workspace, @kolu/* hydrated from the Nix store. Today’s list/watch run unchanged. The foundation lands first so the OpenTUI bundle sits on proven ground.
  • R4.5.2 — OpenTUI render, one endpoint #1479 . Retire list/watch + the columnify text; render a SolidJS-canonical OpenTUI list of one endpoint’s terminals (the already-shipped --socket or single --host, reused verbatim — no new connection capability), with a one-second clock beside it as the liveness proof. The list itself is a one-shot snapshot; live deltas are R4.5.3’s. --json dumps the awareness array.
  • R4.5.3 — the multi-host fleet board #1486 . Fan the one-shot dial over N hosts, mirror each awareness collection into one aggregate keyed by (host, terminalId), go live (repaint on any mirror delta), float every awaiting_user agent to the top across the fleet, and render per-host groups + a breathing alert strip + honest unreachable/skew/empty states.
  • R4.5.4 — the live green dot + full-surface mirror #1497 . @kolu/arivu-contract grows its first activity stream (terminals moving bytes right now, from kaval’s raw output tap); the board paints each a live green dot and drives the whole surface — version cell + awareness + activity — through one mirrorRemoteSurface call. Contract bumped 0.1 → 0.2 (additive); rode surface.md’s drishti merge-gate.
arivu-tui fleet⟳ live · 1s
●2 agents need you— zest · pu-build-7
▌zest· 4 terminals
●claudekolu · feat/dial-ssh #1412 ✓awaiting you3s
◜codexdrishti · masterworking0s
◜claudeanyforge · fix/checks #1408 ✗working4s
○claudenotes · mainidle12m
▌pu-build-7· 2 terminals
●claudekolu · fix/heap-oom #1427 ✓awaiting you9s
◜—infra · deployworking1s
▌staging· unreachableECONNREFUSED
▌local· 1 terminal
○claudearivu · feat/fleetidle2m
● 2 need you◜ 3 working○ 2 idle1 host down

When nothing needs you the board sits calm — cyan working spinners, dim idle rows, a quiet all clear ✓. The moment an agent hits awaiting_user its row lifts to the top of the fleet and a warm amber strip breathes, so a glance tells you someone’s waiting before you’ve read a word. Rows sort needs-you first across the whole fleet; --by agent regroups into one fleet-wide “who is waiting on input, anywhere” list; --json emits the flat [{ host, terminalId, ...AwarenessValue }] for a notifier. Unreachable / skew / empty hosts each render distinctly rather than silently vanishing.

arivu-tui fleet⟳ live · 1s
▌zest· 4 terminals
◜codexdrishti · masterworking0s
◜claudeanyforge · fix/checks #1408 ✗working4s
○claudenotes · mainidle12m
▌pu-build-7· 2 terminals
◜claudekolu · fix/heap-oom #1427 ✓working9s
◜—infra · deployworking1s
▌staging· unreachableECONNREFUSED
▌local· 1 terminal
○claudearivu · feat/fleetidle2m
● 0 need you◜ 4 working○ 2 idleall clear ✓

Host is stamped at the dial site, never by the daemon — the awareness-layer echo of kolu’s kolu-side location stamp (AwarenessValue deliberately carries no hostId). The render reads the aggregate keyed by (host, terminalId), so two boxes’ identical terminal ids stay distinct.

Why this stays a TUI, and what is electricity vs. a leaf

The fleet board is the awareness analog of drishti. Per electricity, @kolu/surface proved it was real electricity by graduating to a second consumer (drishti’s fleet process-monitor). arivu’s fleet is the exact same shape one layer up — a dashboard that mirrors N remote arivu daemons into one host-keyed view, proving arivu’s own surface is itself a receptacle a different app plugs into. The clincher: the fan-out + aggregation needed no contract change to exist (R4.5.3 mirrored the same awareness collection across N hosts). That invariance under a brand-new multi-host consumer is the electricity proof. (R4.5.4’s activity stream came later, as an additive feature, not a tax the multi-host consumer levied.)

Two boundary calls keep it honest:

  • The renderer is electricity — but it already exists as OpenTUI. A domain-agnostic Solid→terminal capability, so R4.5 consumes OpenTUI per prefer-libraries rather than minting a @kolu/solid-tui.
  • The aggregation join is a leaf reducer, not electricity. It hides a bounded host-keyed merge, not a hard volatility (transport + provision + reconnect already belong to @kolu/surface-nix-host; the per-primitive mirror to @kolu/surface). So no @kolu/fleet-mirror — that is exactly the “tidy generic helper” trap electricity warns against.

It stays a TUI, not a surface-app

A read-only aggregator has nothing to fold and nothing to persist, so a browser shell with a stale-tab/reconnect story is pure ceremony for a glance view. The TUI ships fast, is dev-loop-native, and proves the aggregation core cheaply. A web arivu-board (the literal drishti twin — browser ↔ Bun ↔ N ssh dials) is an attractive follow-on, explicitly second, never the headline. And a team-wide read-only monitor is a different product from kolu’s own-canvas fold (remote-terminals R7) — so it belongs as arivu’s own consumer, with zero kolu-server, not folded into kolu.

Why Bun, contained to the viewer

OpenTUI is the production-grade TUI framework (it powers OpenCode and terminal.shop) and ships a Solid reconciler (@opentui/solid), so the dashboard is authored in the same fine-grained-reactive model kolu already lives in. The catch — and the reason the runtime swap came first — is that OpenTUI’s renderer is a native Zig core loaded via Bun.dlopen: it needs Bun.

So the split is narrow and deliberate: arivu-tui alone becomes a Bun binary; everything else, including the arivu daemon, stays Node. The daemon is the half that travels over ssh and leans on node:sqlite, so it must stay Node (and stays cross-arch). The viewer is never provisioned over ssh, so the Bun runtime + per-arch Zig lib live only in the local closure. The packaging is reused, not new ground — it is drishti’s bun2nix recipe wholesale.

OpenTUI + Solid build gotchas (learned the hard way in R4.5.2)

  • OpenTUI repaints only the CHANGED CELL. Verify “is it live?” with raw-PTY capture (frame N+1 ≠ frame N), never snapshot | grep or a glance — both read a live UI as frozen.
  • Bundle solid-js; do NOT mark it external. @opentui/solid’s plugin redirects solid-js to its reactive client build only when solid is loaded during the build. Marking it external ships the non-reactive server build and every live value silently freezes at runtime (invisible in dev, where the bunfig.toml preload redirects at load time). Use external: ["@opentui/core"] only.
  • Pre-bundle; don’t run from source. build.ts (Bun.build + @opentui/solid/bun-plugin) applies babel-preset-solid at build time; clean its outdir first (Bun.build leaves orphan chunks). The smoke that catches the freeze is running the built dist/bin.js and confirming a repaint.
  • SolidJS-canonical only — a live value is createSignal + onMount(setInterval) + onCleanup(clearInterval), read in JSX. No fps caps, requestRender, or process.stderr monkeypatching.
  • Forwarded daemon stderr over --host is quieted at the daemon (warn over --stdio) so it can’t corrupt the alt-screen; the held-open fleet dials route their logs through @kolu/surface-nix-host’s onLog sink.

History

  • The standalone story shipped end-to-end R4.1–R4.5: extract → daemon → remote viewer → Bun → OpenTUI → live fleet → live activity. Supersedes two closed plans ( #1398 , #1409 ) that kept awareness inside kolu and split hairs over an in-process compose-vs-serve boundary this design dissolves by moving the producer into a daemon.
  • R4.4 → dialAgentOnce — the gauntlet lifted the shared one-shot dial into @kolu/surface-nix-host; kaval-tui and arivu-tui are now thin wrappers over one primitive (paired drishti pin-bump merge-gate).
  • R4.5.3 as-built — the board is always live (--json is the one-shot); a dropped one-shot dial flips a host to unreachable rather than reconnecting (long-lived reconnect stays remote-terminals R7 / HostSession); host discovery is --host + a small ~/.ssh/config enumerator (no new dep). Gauntlet + dogfooding added --kaval <host>=<socket> pinning, a responsive repo·branch layout, the --host stderr-bleed fix (onLog sink), and a darwin bun install --backend=copyfile fix.
  • Packaging co-located ( #1491 ) — the viewer’s Bun/Nix manifest moved from nix/packages/ into packages/arivu-tui/nix/ so the viewer is one self-contained directory (a pnpm-workspace exclusion keeps the bun manifest out of pnpm’s glob).

Referenced by

  • Kaval sessions — dial daemons, multiplex the canvas
  • Remote terminals over SSH
